sourceMovie.title: We Were Soldiers
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: We Were Soldiers (2002) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Randall Wallace's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and history blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Great Raid
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If We Were Soldiers clicked for you, The Great Raid (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and history, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after We Were Soldiers?
faq.answer: Try The Great Raid, Danger Close: The Battle of Long Tan, Platoon—each shares DNA with We Were Soldiers while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is We Were Soldiers?
faq.answer: We Were Soldiers (2002) is best described as Action, History, War. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is We Were Soldiers worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
